query: our renderer hand-writes a barrier before and after every pass and it breaks every time we reorder passes — I want passes to just declare what they read and write and have ordering and transitions figured out for us
rationale: direct: render/frame graph deriving order + barriers from declared reads/writes, API-neutral

query: we have a g-buffer, an SSAO target, and a bloom mip chain all alive at once and VRAM is tight — can we reuse the same memory for targets that aren't used at the same time
rationale: implicit: transient render-target aliasing by disjoint lifetime, no API named

query: every time we use a new material the frame hitches for a few ms; I think it's compiling the graphics state on first draw — how do we get rid of the stall
rationale: architecture: precompiled + cached pipeline-state objects, warm the cache, API-neutral

query: we're calling the allocator once per texture and once per mesh and it falls over on big levels — restructure how we hand out GPU memory
rationale: gpu-memory-strategy: sub-allocate from few large blocks, allocation-count cap, strategy not API calls

query: the CPU and GPU keep stalling on each other each frame; we have one set of uniform buffers and one command pool shared across frames — fix the frame pacing
rationale: frames-in-flight: per-frame resource sets + fence so CPU doesn't outrun GPU

query: we want one big array of all our textures that the shader indexes by an integer instead of binding a group per draw
rationale: bindless binding model indexed by handle, frequency grouping; concept not one API

query: how should I think about the stage and access scopes plus a layout transition needed to read a render target right after writing it as a color attachment — what's the general model
rationale: synchronization model: producer/consumer barrier scopes + image-layout transition, conceptual

query: we have 40 feature combinations for our lit shader and build times exploded; should we compile a variant per combo or branch in one ubershader, and how do we recompile on save without restarting
rationale: shader-system: permutations vs ubershader + specialization constants, hot-reload

query: give me the exact VkImageMemoryBarrier2 with srcStageMask/srcAccessMask and the VkImageLayout transition and the vkCmdPipelineBarrier2 call for a color-attachment-to-sampled read
rationale: near-miss: concrete Vulkan Vk*/vkCmd* how-to -> gpu-rendering-vulkan-guide, not the agnostic model

query: set up a three.js scene with orbit controls and a glTF model and some postprocessing passes
rationale: near-miss: high-level web 3D -> threejs-guide, no explicit-API architecture concerns

query: restructure this CPU particle array from AoS to SoA so the update loop stops missing cache
rationale: near-miss: CPU-only data layout -> data-oriented-design-guide

query: how do I architect uploads of static GPU-resident data versus dynamic per-frame data — staging into a device-local tier for one, a persistent-mapped ring for the other
rationale: validation positive: gpu-memory-strategy tiers + staging + ring buffer, API-neutral

query: I want to record draw calls from several worker threads at once and then submit them in order — how should I structure per-thread per-frame recording contexts
rationale: validation positive: per-thread contexts, primary/secondary, multi-threaded recording, conceptual

query: explain the model for replacing a pile of binary cross-queue waits between graphics and async-compute with a single monotonic timeline value we can also wait on from the CPU
rationale: validation positive: timeline value generalizing cross-queue waits + CPU waits, concept

query: walk me through the concrete vkCreateSwapchainKHR + vkAcquireNextImageKHR + vkQueuePresentKHR calls and the VkFence per frame for my Vulkan loop
rationale: validation negative: concrete Vulkan swapchain how-to -> gpu-rendering-vulkan-guide

query: I want my vertex shader to read positions straight out of a big storage buffer instead of binding a vertex layout, so I can support quantized formats and morph targets from one shader
rationale: vertex-assembly-skinning: programmable vertex pull behind a loader, custom packing, morph targets

query: our characters animate but the bone count per vertex varies a lot and a fixed 4-bone vertex format wastes memory — how should I store skin influences and do the blend on the GPU
rationale: vertex-assembly-skinning: indirected influence list (count+offset), in-shader linear blend skinning

query: we have a million particles and uploading them every frame and reading results back is killing us — I want the whole simulation to live on the GPU and drive its own draw count
rationale: gpu-compute-simulation: GPU-resident state, compute update, indirect draw from GPU-tracked count

query: how do I structure a compute-based particle system so spawning and updating are separate dispatches over GPU-resident ring buffers without CPU readback stalls
rationale: validation positive: gpu-compute-simulation event-triggered stages, ring buffer, no readback

query: I'm adding HDR10 output — which swapchain color space and format do I pick, and where do I apply the Rec.2020 conversion and PQ curve relative to tone mapping and UI?
rationale: hdr-output: PQ/Rec.2020 swapchain, linear pipeline, own final color-space + transfer encode

query: my HDR image looks washed out and dim and the UI text blends wrong — I think I'm double-applying a transfer function with an sRGB backbuffer
rationale: validation positive: hdr-output encode-once, paper-white scaling, no auto-sRGB backbuffer for HDR

rationale: near-miss: CPU-only data layout -> data-oriented-design-guide, not GPU simulation
